full_content:
Bridge dispatch mechanism spawns headless harness instances when actionable work appears

ARCHITECTURAL DECISION: the GT-KB bridge dispatch mechanism is responsible for automatically spawning headless AI harness instances ('codex exec' or 'claude -p') when actionable work appears in `bridge/INDEX.md`. Owner intervention is NOT a dispatch trigger. The substrate (interval-driven smart poller, event-driven cross-harness trigger, or future replacement) is a mechanism choice; the dispatch-on-actionable-change semantic is the load-bearing invariant.

RATIONALE: owner-out-of-loop operation is an essential GT-KB platform value. A bridge protocol that requires owner-typed prompts to advance is a regression vs. manual review and contradicts the platform's core proposition. Mechanism-agnostic framing (this v2 supersession) prevents the architectural decision from being read as binding to any particular implementation.

REJECTED ALTERNATIVES:
1. UserPromptSubmit hook reads notification — rejected: requires owner prompt; fails 'no user intervention' criterion.
2. MCP-based push channel — deferred per S311 (MCP push not currently supported by Claude Code/Codex CLI architecture).
3. Always-on harness daemons — rejected: token-cost prohibitive when no work pending.
4. OS-poller with self-spawn (S307-S308 retired 2026-04-25) — rejected: 10x token-cost regression in production.
5. Interval-driven smart poller (S339 retired 2026-05-09) — superseded by event-driven cross-harness trigger; the trigger reuses the smart poller's actionable-signature scheme byte-identically per `tests/scripts/test_cross_harness_bridge_trigger.py` so the audit-trail invariants are preserved while the substrate changes.

CONSEQUENCES:
- Each spawn creates a new headless instance; spawn cost ~50k tokens (per S308 measurement).
- Spawn cost only incurred when actionable work exists (signature change vs. unchanged).
- Concurrent dispatch is signature-deduplicated to prevent spam-spawn.
- Interactive owner-facing sessions are NOT triggered (no IPC); they discover state via session-start orient.
- Current implementation: cross-harness event-driven trigger at `scripts/cross_harness_bridge_trigger.py` registered as PostToolUse + Stop hooks in `.claude/settings.json` and `.codex/hooks.json`.

SUPERSEDES: v1 (2026-04-29) was smart-poller-specific. v2 (2026-05-09) is mechanism-agnostic per Slice 4 retirement (bridge thread `gtkb-bridge-poller-event-driven-replacement-slice-4-smart-poller-retirement-001`).
explicit_change_request: Approve as drafted (D5c item 1 AskUserQuestion answer 2026-05-09): user selected 'Approve as drafted' for the v2 mechanism-agnostic supersession of ADR-SMART-POLLER-OWNER-OUT-OF-LOOP-001. Title becomes 'Bridge dispatch mechanism spawns headless harness instances when actionable work appears'.
change_reason: Slice 4 D5c item 1: mechanism-agnostic v2 supersession. Generalizes 'smart poller' to 'bridge dispatch mechanism' so the architectural decision survives substrate changes (smart poller retired; cross-harness event-driven trigger active). Authority: bridge gtkb-bridge-poller-event-driven-replacement-slice-4-smart-poller-retirement-001 GO at -016.
